items.hook: Guinean President Alpha Condé survives an attempted assassination and coup d'état at his residence in Conakry.
items.body: Guinea, officially the Republic of Guinea, is a coastal country located in West Africa. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Each issue of The Arkham Collector had an approximate print run of 2,500 copies.
items.body: The Arkham Collector was an American fantasy, horror fiction and poetry magazine first published in Summer 1967. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: ‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

items.hook: World War II: The Second Happy Time of Hitler's submarines comes to an end, as the increasingly effective American convoy system compels them to return to the central Atlantic.
items.body: The Second Happy Time was a phase in the Battle of the Atlantic during which Axis submarines attacked merchant shipping and Allied naval vessels along the east coast of North America. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Great Northern War: A numerically superior Polish-Saxon army of Augustus II the Strong, operating from an advantageous defensive position, is defeated by a Swedish army half its size under the command of King Charles XII in the Battle of Klissow.
items.body: In the Great Northern War (1700–1721) a coalition led by Russia successfully contested the supremacy of Sweden in Northern, Central and Eastern Europe. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: The largely autobiographical novel The Ordeal of Gilbert Pinfold by Evelyn Waugh was published.
items.body: An autobiographical novel, also known as an autobiographical fiction, fictional autobiography, or autobiographical fiction novel, is a type of novel which uses autofiction techniques, or the merging of autobiographical and fictive elements. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Leo Leuppi (1893–1972) was a Swiss painter, graphic artist, sculptor, and he was a representative of the Zürcher Schule der Konkreten.
items.body: He was a founder of the avant-garde artists' associations Groupe Suisse Abstraction et Surréalisme and Allianz. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: He died on 25 February 2022, at the age of 89.
items.body: Michel Le Royer was a French actor, known for his roles in La Fayette, Nutty, Naughty Chateau, and Her Harem. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Coinage of Aesernia concerns coins minted in Aesernia, a city in Samnium, where a Roman colony was created in 263 BC.
items.body: The coins were minted around 263 to 240 B.C., after the colony was founded. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Field Marshal Kodandera Madappa Cariappa was an Indian military officer and diplomat who was the Indian Commander-in-Chief (C-in-C) of the Indian Army.
items.body: He led Indian forces on the Western Front during the Indo-Pakistani War of 1947. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Bristol University's Radio Station (Burst) is a radio station run by students of the University of Bristol, UK.
items.body: Its studios are located within the University of Bristol Students' Union building and it broadcasts online. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Brenda Fricker obituary
items.body:
Actor who was the first Irish woman to win an Oscar for her role in the 1989 film classic My Left Foot

Brenda Fricker, who has died aged 81, was only the second Irish actor – and the first female one – to win an Oscar, for her role

items.hook: Taiping Rebellion: Third Battle of Nanking: The Qing dynasty finally defeats the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.
items.body: The Taiping Rebellion, also known as the Taiping Civil War, Revolution, or Movement, was a civil war in late imperial China between the Qing dynasty and the rebel Taiping Heavenly Kingdom. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The British Medical Association is founded as the Provincial Medical and Surgical Association by Sir Charles Hastings at a meeting in the Board Room of the Worcester Infirmary.
items.body: The British Medical Association (BMA) is a registered trade union and professional body for doctors in the United Kingdom. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Could AI be conscious?
items.body:
Experts believe it’s at least possible. We urgently need a plan to navigate the ethical implications

items.hook: It was originally established in 1984 under the title Journal of Welsh Ecclesiastical History.
items.body: The Journal of Welsh Religious History is published by the University of Wales Press on behalf of the Centre for the Advanced Study of Religion in Wales and previously the Welsh Religious History Society. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
items.body: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

items.hook: Sebastian Kunjukunju Bhagavathar was a Malayalam theatre actor, singer, and author.
items.body: He is known for his contributions to Malayalam sangeetha natakam. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".
